evals.prompt: We need to show live transcript text appearing word-by-word as the ML service processes audio. The client only needs to receive data, not send it. The transcript updates are append-only. Which real-time protocol should we use and why?
evals.expected_output: Recommends SSE as the simplest fit for a unidirectional, append-only stream, with justification for why WebSockets or WebTransport are unnecessary here.
assertions: Recommends SSE (or explains why a simpler protocol fits) rather than defaulting to WebSockets.
Justifies the choice based on the unidirectional, append-only nature of the data.
Addresses reconnection and replay for missed transcript segments.
Explains what requirements would warrant upgrading to WebSockets or WebTransport.

evals.prompt: Users can edit meeting notes collaboratively. When User A edits a note, the change is saved via a REST mutation and also broadcast to all connected clients via WebSocket. User A sees their own edit appear twice — once from the optimistic update and once from the WebSocket broadcast. Design the echo suppression mechanism.
evals.expected_output: Designs a source-aware echo suppression pattern using origin IDs or mutation correlation IDs to filter self-originated events.
assertions: Uses a correlation ID, origin ID, or client ID to identify self-originated events.
Filters or reconciles the WebSocket broadcast against the pending optimistic update.
Handles the case where the server-confirmed state differs from the optimistic update (rollback).
Keeps the echo suppression logic on the client side without requiring server changes to filter per-connection.

evals.prompt: Our WebSocket connection for live meeting updates drops when users switch WiFi networks or go through tunnels. Currently, reconnection just re-establishes the socket but doesn't recover missed events. Users see stale data until they refresh the page. Design a resilient reconnection strategy.
evals.expected_output: Designs reconnection with sequence-based replay, handling duplicate detection and gap recovery.
assertions: Includes sequence numbers or timestamps on events to track position.
Replays missed events from the last known position on reconnection.
Handles duplicate events that arrive during the replay window (idempotency).
Addresses the case where the replay buffer has expired and a full state resync is needed.
